nodes.label: EGFR
nodes.detail: Pilot target for EGFR mutation-positive NSCLC.

nodes.label: EGFR mutation-positive NSCLC
nodes.detail: Disease context selected by the user.

nodes.label: Conservative EGFR activity floor
nodes.source: ChEMBL pChEMBL convention; operational early-lead triage policy
nodes.rationale: pChEMBL 7 corresponds to 100 nM when derived from IC50/Ki/Kd in molar units. Target-SAFE uses the lower prediction bound, not the mean, to avoid overclaiming.

nodes.label: Minimum nearest-analog similarity
nodes.source: Target-SAFE applicability-domain calibration on the EGFR reference set
nodes.rationale: Below this level, the candidate is treated as outside the analog evidence domain. Live ChEMBL training can replace this static floor with a scaffold-split calibration.

nodes.label: Minimum graph evidence support for Go
nodes.source: Target-SAFE graph completeness policy
nodes.rationale: Go requires at least moderate tool-grounded support. Lower values keep the candidate in Hold until assay, analog, or external evidence is added.

nodes.label: Extreme logP cap
nodes.source: Lipinski rule-of-five context with a relaxed early-lead triage margin
nodes.rationale: High lipophilicity can drive ADMET risk; this cap prevents confident Go calls.

nodes.label: Extreme molecular weight cap
nodes.source: Lipinski rule-of-five context with a relaxed kinase-inhibitor triage margin
nodes.rationale: Used only as an extreme hard gate; softer MW concerns remain in Hold uncertainty.

nodes.label: Maximum acceptable activity uncertainty
nodes.source: Target-SAFE uncertainty policy
nodes.rationale: A broad interval means the model is not precise enough for Go. The candidate can still remain Hold when the mean looks promising.

nodes.label: Very low QED floor
nodes.source: RDKit QED implementation of Bickerton et al. Quantifying the chemical beauty of drugs
nodes.rationale: A very low QED is treated as a hard early-triage warning rather than a potency claim.

nodes.label: Synthetic accessibility risk cap
nodes.source: Ertl-Schuffenhauer synthetic accessibility score interpretation; Target-SAFE fallback policy
nodes.rationale: High SA candidates should not be advanced without synthetic route review.

nodes.label: Extreme TPSA cap
nodes.source: Veber oral bioavailability context with relaxed early-lead triage margin
nodes.rationale: Used as a conservative hard gate for very polar candidates in this oral TKI pilot.

nodes.label: Interstitial lung disease / pneumonitis
nodes.detail: Use as a checklist item, not as a candidate-specific safety conclusion.

nodes.label: QT prolongation and cardiotoxicity monitoring
nodes.detail: Requires follow-up assay or label review for specific products.

nodes.label: Dermatologic and gastrointestinal adverse reactions
nodes.detail: Useful for clinical-risk context, not for structural toxicity prediction.
